Operational Incident Report – UAE PDPL and Market Conduct Considerations
Entity: Sand Dunes Fintech Services LLC (DIFC Branch) — Ref: OIR-ADGM-26-009
Summary: A tabletop cyber drill in the ADGM environment identified an inadvertently open file share; no production systems or client environments were accessed, and operations continued without interruption.
PDPL note (training): Staff were reminded not to store Emirates ID images or family-origin details on shared drives; onboarding forms now default to minimal data capture.
Generic form labels (placeholders only): Name, Emirates ID [000-0000-0000000-0], Trade Licence [TL-AD-000000]; these fields were used in test datasets and do not relate to any individual or licensee.
ESG operations: The climate dashboard used publicly available emirate energy authority tariff tables and internal utility estimates for an educational display; it does not constitute investor guidance.
Crypto education: Marketing circulated a “CryptoSafe Academy” primer that references VARA and SCA rulebooks with hypothetical tokens; no client identifiers or issuer specifics were included.
Conduct training: Weekly briefing covered insider-list hygiene, information-barrier rooms, blackout reminders, and anti-tipping scenarios using retail promotion examples; all scenarios were generic and already published in our staff handbook.
Non-public client notice (restricted): Marhaba Logistics PJSC (ADX-listed, fictional) informed our DIFC advisory team of a detected cyber incident; pending SCA/ADX disclosure, we created an issuer insider list and imposed an internal trading blackout for personnel assigned to that mandate.
Segregation: The client notice is ring-fenced under an information-barrier and only the designated advisory team has access; marketing and product units were not briefed.
PDPL handling (limited positive finding): During the drill, a legacy backup folder was found to contain historical scans of Emirates ID cards for office visitors; files were quarantined and deleted, and a UAE Data Office notification template was prepared for legal review if required.
Public/stale items: Our 2026-04-01 service latency was previously posted on our website and to the status page and requires no insider treatment.
Commercial terms (templates): Term sheet exemplars referenced “performance fee” and “rebate” headings without prices, amounts, dates, or counterparties; documents were used for training only.
Licensing and registers: Internal register fields include DIFC registered number [DIFC-00-0000] and ADGM licence ref [ADGM-L-XYZ-000]; values shown here are invalid placeholders.
Contact: governance@sd-fintech.example (role mailbox) for incident queries; employees should use the Service Desk portal for ticketing.
Compliance check: Reviewed against UAE PDPL, DIFC DP Law, ADGM FSRA and DFSA conduct rules; no cross-border personal data transfers occurred, and references to crypto, ESG, and cyber topics were for education and operations only.
